items.title: Cross-Project Knowledge Base
items.description: Index LANDSCAPE.md, PAPERS.md, and SUMMARY.md files across all GRD projects on disk into a shared knowledge base. When starting a new survey or deep-dive, automatically surface relevant findings from past projects. Solves the pain point where researchers re-discover the same papers and re-learn the same lessons across projects.

items.title: Contextual Paper Recommendations
items.description: After each phase execution, analyze the SUMMARY.md and suggest 3-5 arXiv papers that are directly relevant to what was built or the problems encountered. Uses the phase goal, metrics, and key decisions as signals. Solves the 'I didn't know this paper existed' problem that leads to reinventing approaches.

items.title: Formal Hypothesis Tracker
items.description: Add a structured hypothesis workflow: state a hypothesis before a phase, define success criteria, then automatically link eval results back to confirm or refute it. Track hypothesis status (proposed/testing/confirmed/refuted) in STATE.md. Solves the problem where researchers lose track of what they were actually trying to prove.

items.title: Claude API Cost Per Phase
items.description: Track estimated token usage and API cost per phase and milestone. Show cost breakdown in the dashboard and emit warnings when a phase is burning budget faster than expected. Solves the surprise invoice problem that plagues teams running autonomous multi-phase workflows.

items.title: Experiment Registry with Diff Views
items.description: Track every experiment run with its config, metrics, and git commit hash in a structured EXPERIMENTS.md. Provide a /grd:compare-experiments command that renders a side-by-side table of any two experiment runs. Solves the 'what was different about that run that worked?' pain point.

items.title: Experiment Tracker Integration (MLflow / W&B)
items.description: When eval results are logged, automatically push metrics to MLflow or Weights & Biases. Read run URLs back and embed them in EVAL.md. Solves the disconnect between GRD's structured workflow and the experiment visualization tools ML teams already use.

items.title: Automated Phase Retrospective
items.description: After phase completion, generate a structured retrospective: what worked, what didn't, time vs estimate accuracy, metric targets hit/missed, and one recommendation for the next phase. Solves the 'we never debrief' problem that causes teams to repeat mistakes.

items.title: Natural Language Phase Creation
items.description: Let users describe a phase in plain English ('add a phase to speed up inference by 2x using quantization') and have GRD generate a properly structured phase entry in ROADMAP.md with goal, success criteria, verification level, and duration estimate. Reduces the friction of getting ideas into the system.

items.title: Multi-Project Dashboard
items.description: A /grd:workspace command that scans a parent directory for all GRD projects and renders an aggregate dashboard: active milestones, phase completion rates, blocked projects, and recent activity. Solves the context-switching overhead for engineers managing multiple R&D threads.

items.title: Proactive Blocker Prediction
items.description: Before a phase starts, analyze its plan against the current codebase, existing blockers in STATE.md, and historical failure patterns to predict likely blockers. Output a risk score and specific warnings. Solves the 'we hit a wall on day 3' problem that derails sprint planning.

items.title: Slack / Discord Phase Notifications
items.description: Push notifications to a configured webhook when phases complete, when blockers are added, or when eval targets are missed. Include a summary of what happened and a link to the relevant planning artifact. Solves the async coordination problem on teams where the autonomous executor runs unattended.

items.title: Semantic Search Across Planning Artifacts
items.description: A /grd:search 'query' command that does semantic (not just keyword) search across all .planning/ markdown files. Returns ranked results with snippets. Solves the 'I know we decided something about this three phases ago but I can't find it' problem.

items.title: Phase Template Library
items.description: A library of reusable phase templates for common R&D patterns (benchmark evaluation, hyperparameter sweep, ablation study, literature review, dataset curation). /grd:add-phase --template ablation-study generates a correctly structured phase with pre-filled success criteria. Reduces blank-page friction.

items.title: Decision Audit Trail
items.description: Every design decision logged via `state add-decision` gets indexed with the phase it was made in, the alternatives considered, and the rationale. A /grd:decisions list command with filtering by topic or phase. Solves the 'why did we choose X over Y?' question that plagues long-running projects.

items.title: Metric Regression Detection
items.description: After each eval report, compare metrics against the BASELINE.md. If any tracked metric regresses beyond a configurable threshold, automatically create a blocker in STATE.md and optionally notify via webhook. Solves the silent regression problem where performance quietly degrades across phases.

items.title: Documentation Export (Confluence / Notion)
items.description: Export a milestone's planning artifacts as a structured document to Confluence or Notion: research findings, decisions, eval results, and final verification report. Solves the 'now write this up for stakeholders' step that researchers dread after completing R&D work.

items.title: Gradual Autonomy with Preference Learning
items.description: Track which confirmation prompts users routinely approve vs reject across sessions. Surface a 'you always approve X, want to automate it?' suggestion after 5+ approvals. Progressively expand YOLO scope based on demonstrated trust. Solves the tedium of re-approving the same low-risk actions repeatedly.

items.title: Auto-Generated PR Descriptions from Phase Plans
items.description: When a phase branch is ready to merge, automatically generate a rich PR description from the phase's PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, and eval results. Includes what was built, why, what was measured, and links to relevant planning artifacts. Eliminates the worst part of the merge process.

items.title: Compute Budget Planning
items.description: Let users annotate phases with estimated GPU hours / API calls. Track actuals from eval runs. Show a milestone-level compute budget view: estimated vs actual, remaining budget, and projected overage. Solves the 'we ran out of credits halfway through' problem on teams with fixed compute budgets.

items.title: AI Peer Review for Research Plans
items.description: A /grd:peer-review phase N command that spawns an adversarial agent to critique the phase's research methodology, flag confounders, question baselines, and suggest missing ablations — simulating a tough academic reviewer. Catches methodological flaws before expensive execution.

items.title: Changelog Auto-Writer
items.description: Generate a human-readable CHANGELOG entry for a completed milestone by synthesizing all phase SUMMARY.md files. Output user-facing language (not implementation jargon). Solves the 'writing changelogs is tedious and always delayed' problem that leads to sparse or missing release notes.

items.title: Phase Dependency Impact Analysis
items.description: When a phase is delayed or its scope changes, automatically compute which downstream phases are affected, by how many days, and what their revised start dates would be. Show a cascading impact view. Solves the 'one slipped phase ruins the whole milestone' planning problem.

items.title: Experiment Reproducibility Checker
items.description: Before marking a phase as verified, check that all experiments in EVAL.md have: a pinned random seed, pinned dependency versions, and a documented dataset split. Flag missing reproducibility artifacts as verification blockers. Solves the embarrassing 'I can't reproduce my own results' problem.

items.title: Phase-Level Team Assignment
items.description: Annotate phases and plans with assignees (GitHub usernames). When syncing to GitHub Issues or Jira, set issue assignees automatically. Show an assignment heatmap in the dashboard to surface overloaded team members. Solves the visibility gap on teams where multiple people contribute to a milestone.

items.title: R&D Knowledge Graph
items.description: Build a graph linking papers → decisions → phases → metrics → outcomes. Export as a Mermaid diagram or interactive HTML. Answers 'which paper led to which implementation decision that drove which metric improvement?' Solves the provenance problem that makes R&D hard to explain to others.

items.title: Phase Failure Autopsy
items.description: When a phase ends with missed eval targets or unresolved blockers, run an autopsy agent that reads all artifacts and produces a structured root cause analysis: what was expected, what happened, most likely cause, and what to try next. Removes the guesswork from 'why did this phase not work?'

items.title: Effort Estimation Calibration
items.description: Track estimated vs actual duration for every completed phase. Surface a calibration multiplier ('your estimates run 1.4x short on implementation phases, 0.9x on research phases'). Use this to auto-adjust future estimates. Solves the universal problem of software effort estimation being systematically wrong.

items.title: Backend Capability Parity Report
items.description: A /grd:backend-parity command that tests which GRD features are available under the current backend (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode) vs Claude Code, and which fall back gracefully. Renders a feature support matrix. Solves the 'does this work on my setup?' confusion when users switch backends.

items.title: Research Gap Detector
items.description: After a survey, compare what LANDSCAPE.md covers against the phase goals in ROADMAP.md. Highlight phases that reference techniques not covered in the survey, flagging them as 'research gaps' that need a deep-dive before planning. Prevents planning phases with insufficient research grounding.

items.title: VS Code Extension for GRD
items.description: A VS Code extension that renders the ROADMAP.md as a visual kanban board, highlights files touched by the active phase, and provides a sidebar for the dashboard. Solves the context-switching overhead between the terminal and the editor when tracking research progress.

items.title: bin/grd-tools.js help string is manually maintained and outdated
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 144-148: the usage string in error() manually lists commands and omits `evolve`, `autopilot`, `worktree-hook-create`, and `worktree-hook-remove` (present in routeCommand at lines 742-747). This is the first thing a confused user sees. Generate the command list dynamically from the routing table or keep it in sync via a test.

items.title: cmdStateLoad raw output missing autonomous_mode, ceremony, and execution fields
items.description: lib/state.js lines 70-91: the --raw format outputs only 12 key=value pairs. Config fields added after initial implementation — autonomous_mode, use_teams, code_review_enabled, ceremony settings — are present in JSON output but absent from raw text. Commands that parse raw output with shell scripting will miss these fields, causing silent misconfiguration.

items.title: STATE.md error messages don't suggest corrective action
items.description: lib/state.js lines 131, 180, 223: multiple catch blocks call `error('STATE.md not found')` with no path hint and no recovery suggestion (e.g., 'run /grd:new-project or /grd:resume to initialize'). The error function at lib/utils.js lines 512-515 is already available — improve the message strings to be actionable.

items.title: cmdSearch only searches .planning/, missing commands/ and agents/ content
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 2627-2653: cmdSearch at line 2632 scans only .planning/ markdown files. Searching for 'token threshold' or 'ceremony level' returns no results if those terms only appear in commands/ or agents/ markdown. Add commands/ and agents/ to the search scope or accept a --path parameter.

items.title: Evolve discovery silently falls back to heuristics when Claude parse fails
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 1232-1244 and 1291-1296: parseDiscoveryOutput() at line 1311 strips markdown fences and retries, but if Claude outputs explanatory text before/after the JSON, parse fails and returns [], triggering the static heuristic fallback at line 1291. The user has no way to know the fallback occurred. Log a warning when Claude-powered discovery falls back to heuristics.

items.title: analyzeComplexity() silently returns empty results when ESLint is unavailable
items.description: lib/cleanup.js lines 80-112: execFileSync('npx', ['eslint', ...]) at line 95 fails with a cryptic error if ESLint is not available. The catch block at line 102 returns [] silently. Users see no complexity violations — not because there are none, but because the tool isn't installed. Log a diagnostic when ESLint cannot run.

items.title: cmdInitResearchWorkflow accepts arbitrary workflow names without validation
items.description: lib/context.js (called from bin/grd-tools.js lines 543-551): cmdInitResearchWorkflow accepts any `wf` string and embeds it into output JSON without validation. While the CLI router validates via validateSubcommand, callers invoking the function directly (e.g., in tests or via MCP) can pass arbitrary workflow names. Add an allowed-workflows check inside the function for defense in depth.

items.title: Context init functions don't detect missing .planning/ directory
items.description: lib/context.js cmdInit* functions (all 20+) attempt to read .planning/ files without first checking if the directory exists. Users on fresh projects receive file-not-found errors instead of a helpful suggestion to run /grd:new-project.

items.title: Phase number error message doesn't explain expected format
items.description: lib/context.js line 97 produces: 'phase required for init execute-phase. Usage: init execute-phase <phase-number>'. Users don't know if phase-number should be '1', '01', '1.1', or '01.1'. Add a concrete example.

items.title: Phase add error message lacks format guidance
items.description: lib/phase.js line 128 produces: 'description required for phase add'. No example is shown. Should say: 'description required (e.g., grd phase add "Implement caching layer")'.

items.title: buildParallelContext() has no JSDoc despite complex signature
items.description: lib/parallel.js lines 55-66, buildParallelContext() takes 3 parameters and returns a complex object used by all parallel execution paths. No JSDoc exists. Undocumented parameter semantics make it hard for new contributors to use correctly.

items.title: inferCeremonyLevel() logic undocumented
items.description: lib/context.js line 46, inferCeremonyLevel() auto-selects 'light'|'standard'|'full' based on opaque signals. No JSDoc explains the thresholds. Users relying on auto-inference have no way to understand why a level was chosen.

items.title: Evolve discovery functions lack JSDoc
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 450-500, discoverUsabilityItems(), discoverStabilityItems(), and related functions have no JSDoc. The discovery engine is complex and new; without documentation the intent of each function is unclear.

items.title: 15+ public functions in backend.js lack JSDoc
items.description: lib/backend.js — detectBackend, resolveBackendModel, getBackendCapabilities have inline comments but no @param/@returns blocks. IDEs can't autocomplete parameters; generated docs incomplete.

items.title: Error messages inconsistently actionable across CLI
items.description: lib/commands.js:100-200 and lib/phase.js:80-150 — Some errors include helpful context ('Run /grd:plan-phase 01 first'), others are terse ('Phase not found'). No consistent severity level naming or actionable guidance pattern.

items.title: --raw flag behavior undocumented with inconsistent formats
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js:100-200 — Many commands support --raw but usage is not documented in help text. Output formats vary (JSON, newline-separated, custom). Scripts and integrations break if format changes.

items.title: paths.js functions imported with inconsistent aliases
items.description: lib/paths.js — Functions like phasesDir, researchDir, todosDir are imported with inline aliases in 15+ places. No documentation on what each path resolves to relative to .planning/. Hard for contributors to understand directory structure.

items.title: cmdResolveModel silently returns 'sonnet' for unknown agent types in --raw mode
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 463-468 — when `agentType` is not in `MODEL_PROFILES`, the function returns `{ model: 'sonnet', unknown_agent: true }` in JSON mode but just `'sonnet'` in `--raw` mode with no warning. A typo in an agent name silently falls back to sonnet.

items.title: flag() helper in grd-tools.js returns the next flag as a value when flag value is missing
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 131-134 — `flag(args, name, fallback)` returns `args[i+1]` without checking if it starts with `--`. For example, `state patch --field --value` would set the field name to `'--value'`. There is no guard producing a clear error message for this malformed invocation.

items.title: cmdHistoryDigest silently skips malformed SUMMARY.md files with no file path logged
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 424-426 — the inner loop catches parse errors with `catch (e) { // Skip malformed summaries }` and no file path or error detail. The outer catch (line 442) gives only a generic message. Debugging a malformed summary requires manual bisection.

items.title: spawnClaudeAsync drops the error detail when the claude binary is missing
items.description: lib/autopilot.js line 216 — `child.on('error', ...)` sets `exitCode: 1` but ignores the error object. The caller (grd-tools.js line 738) catches only `err.message` from the outer rejection. If the `claude` binary is missing or not executable, the user gets no actionable diagnostic.

items.title: Generic 'Phase not found' errors lack recovery hints
items.description: Several commands (lib/phase.js:324, lib/commands.js multiple locations) emit bare 'Phase not found' or 'ROADMAP.md not found' with no actionable guidance. Replace with messages that show available phases, suggest running `grd state load` to diagnose, or indicate what the expected path should be.

items.title: MCP tool descriptions in mcp-server.js lack examples
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js (2,227 lines) registers 30+ tools with description strings. Many descriptions omit parameter examples, valid value ranges, or error conditions. LLM agents calling these tools without examples produce malformed inputs. Add concrete example values to each inputSchema description field.

items.title: Evolve discovery silently returns empty on JSON parse error
items.description: lib/evolve.js discoverWithClaude() parses Claude's response as JSON and catches parse errors silently, returning an empty improvement list. The user gets no feedback that discovery failed. Emit a warning with the raw response excerpt so users can diagnose prompt or model issues.

items.title: cmdPhaseInsert error says 'not found in ROADMAP' when phase is actually shipped
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 195–200: When a phase number exists but is already in the shipped section, the error says 'not found in ROADMAP' instead of 'not in active roadmap'. Users think the phase doesn't exist and re-add it.

items.title: cmdWorktreeRemove reports success when git still holds a reference
items.description: lib/worktree.js lines 316–322: `already_gone: true` is returned when the path doesn't exist on disk, but the function doesn't run `git worktree prune` to remove the stale git reference. Users believe cleanup is complete when it isn't.

items.title: Phase consistency check doesn't distinguish plan-complete vs has-summaries
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 606–680: `cmdValidateConsistency` detects orphaned phases but reports only counts, not whether plans are 'complete' (all summaries match plans) vs 'in-progress' (some summaries present). Reduces actionability of output.

items.title: cmdGenerateSlug doesn't guarantee ASCII-safe output
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 70–85: Generated slugs pass through Unicode normalization but don't strip non-ASCII characters. Slugs used in file paths can produce system-specific results on filesystems with different Unicode normalization modes.

items.title: DIMENSION_WEIGHTS constants have no rationale comments
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 100–180: `DIMENSION_WEIGHTS`, `EFFORT_MODIFIERS`, and `SOURCE_MODIFIERS` are defined without comments explaining the tuning rationale. Contributors can't reason about whether adjusting a weight is correct.

items.title: grd-tools.js help text is hardcoded and incomplete
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 145-147: help output is a static string that doesn't reflect the ~40+ commands actually implemented. Users who run `grd-tools --help` see an incomplete list. Generate help text dynamically from the command routing table.

items.title: postinstall.js creates directories silently
items.description: bin/postinstall.js lines 43-67: directories are created without any log output. Users can't tell if postinstall ran or was skipped. Add per-directory `console.log('  Created .planning/milestones/anonymous')` messages.

items.title: No guidance for recovering from corrupted STATE.md or orphaned phases
items.description: docs/ contains no troubleshooting guide. There is no documentation on recovering from a corrupted STATE.md, fixing orphaned phases after a failed phase add/remove, or resolving worktree conflicts. Add docs/troubleshooting.md with these common recovery scenarios.

items.title: scaffold.js silently falls back to default template on error
items.description: lib/scaffold.js lines 70-77: template selection errors are caught and silently fall back to 'standard'. Users don't know why their specified template was ignored. Change to output the error reason: `{ error: 'Failed to select template: ${e.message}' }` before fallback.

items.title: Dashboard regenerates all data synchronously on every call
items.description: lib/commands.js cmdDashboard and related: ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, and phase summaries are all parsed synchronously on each dashboard invocation. For large projects this creates noticeable lag. Add a TTL-based cache (30s) or a `--no-cache` flag.

items.title: Validation error messages lack actionable context
items.description: lib/utils.js lines 330-424: validation functions throw errors but the messages don't suggest how to fix the problem. E.g., 'Invalid phase number' with no indication of valid range. Add suggested fixes to each validation error: 'Phase N not found. Available phases: 1-5.'

items.title: lib/tracker.js error messages don't guide the user to fix the issue
items.description: lib/tracker.js line 448 outputs error('No tracker configured') without telling the user what to configure or where. Similarly, config parse failures return silently. Better: error('Tracker not configured. Run /grd:tracker-setup or set tracker.provider in .planning/config.json').

items.title: bin/grd-tools.js help text lists 50+ commands with no grouping
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 78-80 output a flat list of all commands without grouping by domain (state, phase, scaffold, tracker, etc.) or including examples. Users cannot quickly find the command they need. Group by category and add one-line examples per command.

items.title: lib/evolve.js work item scoring algorithm is undocumented and DIMENSION_WEIGHTS unused
items.description: lib/evolve.js scoreWorkItem() applies weights to work items but DIMENSION_WEIGHTS is defined and never actually used in the prioritization logic. The scoring algorithm is not documented. Users cannot understand why certain items are selected. Document the algorithm and fix or remove the dead DIMENSION_WEIGHTS constant.

items.title: lib/context.js silently ignores backend detection failure
items.description: lib/context.js line 76 catches backend detection errors with no logging. When backend detection fails, the wrong model capabilities are assumed, leading to incorrect model selection in downstream commands. Should log a warning: 'Backend detection failed, defaulting to minimal capabilities'.

items.title: lib/mcp-server.js tool descriptions lack parameter examples
items.description: COMMAND_DESCRIPTORS in lib/mcp-server.js defines tool descriptions but most lack input parameter examples (e.g., what a valid phase number looks like, what fields state patch accepts). Claude and other MCP clients struggle to form correct calls without examples. Add example values to all parameter descriptions.

items.title: lib/evolve.js core algorithms have no JSDoc
items.description: lib/evolve.js discovery engine, scoring logic (~line 200), and iteration/dependency merging (~line 400+) are documented only with sparse inline comments. The algorithms are non-obvious and would benefit greatly from JSDoc covering params, return values, and algorithm rationale.

items.title: lib/mcp-server.js (2227 lines) lacks architectural overview
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js has no module-level documentation explaining request/response patterns, tool registration strategy, or error handling approach. Maintainers have no way to understand the design without reading the entire file.

items.title: Merge conflicts in worktree abort with no actionable guidance
items.description: lib/worktree.js cmdWorktreeMerge: when a merge conflict causes an abort, the error is surfaced but no actionable guidance is given to the user (e.g., 'run git status in .worktrees/X to resolve manually'). User is left confused about next steps.

items.title: CLAUDE.md has no documentation for the evolve loop
items.description: CLAUDE.md documents all other workflows extensively but has no section for the evolve loop (work item dimensions, discovery algorithm, scoring, iteration count semantics). Users cannot understand how to tune the evolve loop or interpret its output without reading source code.

items.title: Destructive operations lack --dry-run flag
items.description: lib/phase.js:180-250 (cmdPhaseRemove) and lib/worktree.js:300-400 — cmdPhaseRemove, cmdWorktreeRemove, and file writes lack --dry-run option to preview changes before executing. Users can't safely verify what will be deleted.

items.title: Field normalization logic duplicated in cmdStatePatch and cmdStateUpdate
items.description: lib/state.js lines 143-183 vs 192-226: both functions contain identical 10-line blocks for 'try original field name, fall back to underscore-to-space mapping'. Extract into a shared `stateReplaceFieldSafe(content, field, value)` helper. The internal stateReplaceField already exists but only handles exact-match replacement.

items.title: lib/paths.js duplicates milestone-parsing from utils.js due to circular dependency
items.description: lib/paths.js lines 65-123: inferMilestoneFromDisk() and parseRoadmapMilestone() are explicit duplicates of getMilestoneInfo() in lib/utils.js lines 669-707. Comments at lines 59 and 93 acknowledge this: 'Duplicated here to avoid circular dependency: utils.js imports paths.js.' Fix the circular dependency by moving getMilestoneInfo from utils.js to paths.js and importing it from there.

items.title: Custom YAML parser in lib/frontmatter.js mishandles edge cases
items.description: lib/frontmatter.js lines 19-100: the manual stack-based YAML parser handles basic keys, inline arrays, and list items but not multi-level nested objects, quoted strings with colons, or multi-line values. Frontmatter is a core data format used in PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, command files, and agent files. Consider replacing with js-yaml (already common in Node.js) or substantially expanding the parser's test coverage.

items.title: lib/tracker.js internal handlers not exported, limiting unit testability
items.description: lib/tracker.js lines 480, 523, 561: handleSyncRoadmap, handleSyncPhase, handleUpdateStatus, handleAddComment, handleSyncStatus, and handleSchedule are private to cmdTracker dispatch and have no JSDoc. Tracker sync failures are silent by design but bugs in these handlers cause data loss. Exporting and documenting them would allow direct unit testing without going through the full dispatch.

items.title: discoverQualityItems() only checks tests/unit/, causing false positives
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 362-388: the test-file discovery at line 374 only checks `tests/unit/{file}.test.js`. Tests at `tests/*.test.js` (e.g., tests/backend.test.js, tests/commands.test.js) are ignored. This causes false positives — lib/backend.js already has tests/backend-real-env.test.js but discoverQualityItems would still flag it as missing a test file.

items.title: lib/backend.js model name table contains non-existent model identifiers
items.description: lib/backend.js lines 39-47: the DEFAULT_BACKEND_MODELS table maps to `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gemini-3-pro`, and `claude-opus-4-5` / `claude-sonnet-4-5` / `claude-haiku-4-5`. Several of these are not publicly known identifiers as of early 2026. Stale model names cause API errors for non-Claude backends. Add a comment with the date the table was last verified and provide a config override mechanism.

items.title: mcp-server.js branch coverage at 55%
items.description: jest.config.js line 19 sets the branch coverage threshold for lib/mcp-server.js at 55% — the lowest in the codebase. The MCP server handles all JSON-RPC dispatch and error paths; uncovered branches leave failure modes untested.

items.title: phase.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/phase.js at 70%. Phase lifecycle operations (add/insert/remove/complete) are critical; edge cases like inserting into a single-phase roadmap or removing the active phase are likely untested.

items.title: tracker.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/tracker.js at 70%. Tracker integration has multiple providers (github, mcp-atlassian, none) and migration paths; incomplete coverage could miss sync failure branches.

items.title: verify.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/verify.js at 70%. Verification gates are safety-critical; 70% branch coverage is too low for a module that gates phase completion and plan execution.

items.title: evolve.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/evolve.js at 70%. The evolve loop is new and complex; low branch coverage in discovery and work item generation increases risk of silent failures in the autonomous iteration loop.

items.title: scaffold.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/scaffold.js at 70%. Scaffolding errors that create malformed phase directories are difficult to detect after the fact; edge cases in template selection need higher coverage.

items.title: commands.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/commands.js at 70%. With 39 exported functions and complex dashboard rendering logic, the uncovered 30% likely includes error branches in cmdDashboard() and related parsing functions.

items.title: Missing tests for MCP error paths in captureExecution
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js:1868-1972 — captureExecution and captureExecutionAsync intercept process.exit but lack tests for malformed JSON responses, concurrent exits, and nested process calls. Silent failures won't propagate to MCP clients.

items.title: Sparse tests for phase add/insert/remove edge cases
items.description: lib/phase.js:100-300 — cmdPhaseInsert modifies ROADMAP.md at specific heading levels but tests for malformed headings, missing phases, and edge-case insertions are missing. Complex mutations can silently corrupt file structure.

items.title: No tests for tracker sync failure scenarios
items.description: lib/tracker.js:220-350 — GitHub/Jira sync via gh CLI has no tests for network timeouts, malformed configs, or partial sync states. Silent failures leave phase status out of sync without user visibility.

items.title: Config loaded without schema validation
items.description: lib/utils.js:100-250 — loadConfig accepts any JSON and fills in defaults without validating field values (e.g., ceremony level is valid enum, model_profile is supported). Typos in config.json silently use defaults without warning.

items.title: Phase name length and charset not validated
items.description: lib/phase.js:200-300 — normalizePhaseName accepts kebab-case slugs but doesn't validate max length (filesystem limits ~255 chars) or reject emoji/unicode. Very long slugs or special chars cause filesystem or ROADMAP parsing issues.

items.title: No round-trip integrity tests for markdown-split
items.description: lib/markdown-split.js:45-120 — readMarkdownWithPartials has no test that reassembly produces the original content. Silent data corruption in large ROADMAP/STATE file reassembly goes undetected.

items.title: Custom YAML parser in frontmatter.js fails on multi-line strings and embedded colons
items.description: lib/frontmatter.js lines 19-100 — the hand-rolled parser has no support for block scalars (`|`, `>`), multi-line strings, or quoted strings with embedded colons. A PLAN.md with `objective: "Fix the foo: bar issue"` would parse the colon inside quotes as a nested key separator.

items.title: cmdFindPhase duplicates findPhaseInternal from utils.js
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 482-536 re-implements phase directory discovery that already exists in utils.js `findPhaseInternal` (lines 545-620). The two implementations have diverged — bug fixes applied to one don't flow to the other.

items.title: cmdResolveModel returns raw tier name instead of backend-resolved model ID
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 455-473 — `cmdResolveModel` returns the tier string (e.g. `'sonnet'`) without calling `resolveBackendModel`. But `resolveModelInternal` and `resolveModelForAgent` in utils.js do call `resolveBackendModel`. The CLI `resolve-model` command gives different results than the model actually used by agents.

items.title: Git commit failure branch sets wrong reason 'nothing_to_commit'
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 588-594 — the `else` branch for a generic git commit failure (e.g. pre-commit hook rejection) sets `reason: 'nothing_to_commit'`, identical to the actual empty-index case above it. Agents reading this response cannot distinguish an empty index from a hook failure.

items.title: discoverImproveFeatureItems output-call regex generates false positives and negatives
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 689-706 — the regex `output\(\s*(\w+)\s*,\s*raw\s*(?:,\s*raw\s*\?)?\s*\)` only matches single-identifier first arguments (misses object literals), and the follow-up check regex fails on multi-line calls. This heuristic produces unreliable discovery results.

items.title: cmdStateSnapshot raw output can emit 'NaN%' if Progress field contains a bar string
items.description: lib/state.js line 719 — `result.progress_percent` is derived from `parseInt(progressRaw.replace('%', ''), 10)`. If STATE.md Progress field contains a bar like `[██░░] 20%`, `parseInt` of the full bar text returns `NaN`, so the raw output becomes `"Phase 5 (active) - NaN%"`.

items.title: worktree.js hook-create/remove and push-pr paths have no unit test coverage
items.description: lib/worktree.js — `cmdWorktreeHookCreate` and `cmdWorktreeHookRemove` are only reachable via alias commands (grd-tools.js lines 742-746) and appear to have no test coverage in tests/unit/worktree.test.js. The `cmdWorktreePushAndPR` path is similarly untested at the unit level.

items.title: 327 JSDoc blocks missing @throws annotations
items.description: lib/phase.js, lib/commands.js, and lib/context.js have comprehensive @param/@returns JSDoc but zero @throws tags. Functions like cmdPhaseAdd, cmdPhaseRemove can throw multiple error conditions. Without @throws, callers have no contract for error handling.

items.title: TODO/FIXME markers indicating unfinished work
items.description: Multiple TODO/FIXME comments exist across lib/ that flag known deficiencies. Each represents deferred technical debt. Audit and either resolve or convert to tracked issues with /grd:add-todo so they don't get lost across sessions.

items.title: Error paths in lib/deps.js and lib/scaffold.js uncovered
items.description: tests/unit/deps.test.js and tests/unit/scaffold.test.js have good happy-path coverage but minimal tests for failure modes (e.g., npm install failure in deps.js, permission errors in scaffold.js). The per-file thresholds in jest.config.js may be masking branch coverage gaps on catch paths.

items.title: lib/parallel.js error propagation paths not tested
items.description: lib/parallel.js orchestrates concurrent agent execution. tests/unit/parallel.test.js focuses on success cases. Worker crash, timeout, and partial-failure scenarios (where some workers fail and others succeed) lack dedicated test coverage, making the parallel executor brittle under real failure conditions.

items.title: Golden tests use capture.sh but CI enforcement unclear
items.description: tests/golden/ uses a capture.sh script to regenerate snapshots but it's unclear if golden output mismatch fails CI or just warns. If capture.sh must be manually run to update snapshots, developers may commit code that silently changes CLI output. Add explicit golden-test diff check to the test suite that fails on mismatch.

items.title: mcp-server.js has only 55% branch coverage threshold
items.description: jest.config.js line 20: `mcp-server.js` has the lowest branch-coverage threshold in the project at 55%. MCP tool handlers likely have multiple untested error and edge-case branches, making regressions likely.

items.title: commands.js and tracker.js sit at 70% branch coverage
items.description: jest.config.js lines 13, 26: Both modules share the same low threshold. Complex command routing and multi-provider tracker integration lack tests for error paths, concurrent failures, and malformed input.

items.title: No test for git merge conflict in cmdMilestoneComplete
items.description: lib/phase.js lines ~780–1000: `cmdMilestoneComplete` contains git merge logic with an abort path, but the test suite never exercises `git merge --abort`. Merge conflict handling is entirely untested.

items.title: Removing a phase does not check dependent phases
items.description: lib/phase.js line ~331: `cmdPhaseRemove` doesn't check whether the target phase is referenced in other phases' `Depends on:` fields. Removing a dependency silently creates orphaned references in ROADMAP.md.

items.title: No integration tests for stale/locked worktree cleanup
items.description: tests/integration/ has no tests for worktree cleanup when worktrees are stale or locked by another process. These edge cases are common in CI environments. Add integration tests that simulate locked worktrees and verify graceful recovery.

items.title: No integration tests for tracker sync failures
items.description: tests/integration/ lacks tests for tracker sync network failures and retry behavior. The tracker is marked non-blocking but there is no test verifying the workflow continues when GitHub/Jira are unreachable. Add tests with mocked HTTP failures.

items.title: No integration tests for long-term roadmap refine/link/unlink
items.description: The refine, link, and unlink operations in lib/long-term-roadmap.js lack integration test coverage in tests/integration/. These are complex operations that modify multiple files. Add end-to-end tests for each subcommand.

items.title: jest.config.js requires manual threshold entry for each new file
items.description: jest.config.js lines 6-30: per-file coverage thresholds must be manually added for each new module. New files start at 0% until someone adds them. Invert the approach: set a global default threshold and maintain only an exemption list for files with known lower coverage.

items.title: lib/mcp-server.js branch coverage threshold too low at 55%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/mcp-server.js at only 55%. The MCP tool dispatch logic has many conditional branches that go untested, meaning tool dispatch failures could go undetected. Raise threshold to at least 75% and add tests for error paths in executeTool().

items.title: lib/tracker.js GitHub tracker error paths have no tests
items.description: createGitHubTracker() calls ghExec() but no test covers ghExec() timeout, malformed gh CLI output, or rate limit errors. These are real production failure modes. Add unit tests for lib/tracker.js error scenarios.

items.title: lib/autopilot.js timeout and kill-signal handling not tested
items.description: lib/autopilot.js spawnClaudeAsync() has timeout logic but unit tests do not verify timeout behavior or kill signal handling. A hung subprocess could block the entire autopilot loop indefinitely.

items.title: No schema validation for .planning/config.json
items.description: lib/context.js loadConfig() (and callers) return any parsed JSON without validating required fields or types. Invalid config silently produces wrong behavior (e.g., missing ceremony.default_level defaults to undefined). Add Joi/Zod schema validation with helpful error messages.

items.title: lib/frontmatter.js has no schema validation per file type
items.description: frontmatter get/set/merge operations in lib/frontmatter.js accept any YAML without validating required fields per document type (PLAN.md vs SUMMARY.md vs RESEARCH.md). Invalid frontmatter propagates silently and can break downstream tools like verify plan-structure.

items.title: No integration test for lib/markdown-split.js split/reassemble round-trip
items.description: lib/markdown-split.js handles large-file splitting and reassembly, but only unit tests exist. No test verifies that split→reassemble produces identical output for real large planning files. A corruption bug in multi-part reassembly would be silent.

items.title: tests/golden/ snapshots have no documented update procedure
items.description: tests/golden/ captures CLI output via capture.sh but there is no documented or automated way to update golden snapshots when CLI output intentionally changes. Contributors must manually update snapshots, which is error-prone. Add npm run test:golden:update and document in CLAUDE.md.

items.title: lib/parallel.js wave failure aggregation behavior not covered by tests
items.description: lib/parallel.js handles wave-based parallel plan execution with failure aggregation, but integration tests don't cover the case where some plans in a wave succeed and others fail. The partial-success + continue-on-error path has no test coverage, risking silent data loss.

items.title: bin/postinstall.js has no unit tests
items.description: The critical postinstall script that creates directories during npm install has no corresponding test file in tests/. Directory creation failures would only be caught in live CI runs, not during development.

items.title: Evolve loop has no end-to-end integration tests
items.description: tests/integration/ contains no tests covering the evolve workflow's discovery engine, scoring logic, and work item lifecycle. This is one of the most complex orchestration flows in the codebase (lib/evolve.js) and is entirely untested at the integration level.

items.title: lib/cleanup.js calls findJsFiles once per analysis type
items.description: lib/cleanup.js analyzeFileSize, analyzeComplexity, analyzeExportConsistency, and analyzeDocStaleness each call findJsFiles independently. On large projects this means 4–6 full directory walks per quality check. findJsFiles result should be computed once and passed to all analyzers.

items.title: No check for duplicate phase numbers in add/insert
items.description: lib/phase.js cmdPhaseAdd and cmdPhaseInsert do not verify that the new phase number doesn't already exist in ROADMAP.md. Duplicate phase numbers cause crashes downstream in roadmap parsing and context init.

items.title: Quality analysis results are not deduplicated
items.description: lib/cleanup.js runQualityAnalysis: analyzeComplexity may report the same function violation multiple times (e.g., from different ESLint passes). Cleanup plans end up with duplicate tasks, which is confusing and leads to redundant work.

items.title: Improve test coverage for context.js
items.description: lib/context.js has a coverage threshold of 87% lines (target: 90%). Increase test coverage to strengthen code quality.

items.title: Improve test coverage for evolve.js
items.description: lib/evolve.js has a coverage threshold of 85% lines (target: 90%). Increase test coverage to strengthen code quality.

items.title: Cross-Project Knowledge Base
items.description: Index LANDSCAPE.md, PAPERS.md, and SUMMARY.md files across all GRD projects on disk into a shared knowledge base. When starting a new survey or deep-dive, automatically surface relevant findings from past projects. Solves the pain point where researchers re-discover the same papers and re-learn the same lessons across projects.

items.title: Contextual Paper Recommendations
items.description: After each phase execution, analyze the SUMMARY.md and suggest 3-5 arXiv papers that are directly relevant to what was built or the problems encountered. Uses the phase goal, metrics, and key decisions as signals. Solves the 'I didn't know this paper existed' problem that leads to reinventing approaches.

items.title: Formal Hypothesis Tracker
items.description: Add a structured hypothesis workflow: state a hypothesis before a phase, define success criteria, then automatically link eval results back to confirm or refute it. Track hypothesis status (proposed/testing/confirmed/refuted) in STATE.md. Solves the problem where researchers lose track of what they were actually trying to prove.

items.title: Claude API Cost Per Phase
items.description: Track estimated token usage and API cost per phase and milestone. Show cost breakdown in the dashboard and emit warnings when a phase is burning budget faster than expected. Solves the surprise invoice problem that plagues teams running autonomous multi-phase workflows.

items.title: Experiment Registry with Diff Views
items.description: Track every experiment run with its config, metrics, and git commit hash in a structured EXPERIMENTS.md. Provide a /grd:compare-experiments command that renders a side-by-side table of any two experiment runs. Solves the 'what was different about that run that worked?' pain point.

items.title: Experiment Tracker Integration (MLflow / W&B)
items.description: When eval results are logged, automatically push metrics to MLflow or Weights & Biases. Read run URLs back and embed them in EVAL.md. Solves the disconnect between GRD's structured workflow and the experiment visualization tools ML teams already use.

items.title: Automated Phase Retrospective
items.description: After phase completion, generate a structured retrospective: what worked, what didn't, time vs estimate accuracy, metric targets hit/missed, and one recommendation for the next phase. Solves the 'we never debrief' problem that causes teams to repeat mistakes.

items.title: Natural Language Phase Creation
items.description: Let users describe a phase in plain English ('add a phase to speed up inference by 2x using quantization') and have GRD generate a properly structured phase entry in ROADMAP.md with goal, success criteria, verification level, and duration estimate. Reduces the friction of getting ideas into the system.

items.title: Multi-Project Dashboard
items.description: A /grd:workspace command that scans a parent directory for all GRD projects and renders an aggregate dashboard: active milestones, phase completion rates, blocked projects, and recent activity. Solves the context-switching overhead for engineers managing multiple R&D threads.

items.title: Proactive Blocker Prediction
items.description: Before a phase starts, analyze its plan against the current codebase, existing blockers in STATE.md, and historical failure patterns to predict likely blockers. Output a risk score and specific warnings. Solves the 'we hit a wall on day 3' problem that derails sprint planning.

items.title: Slack / Discord Phase Notifications
items.description: Push notifications to a configured webhook when phases complete, when blockers are added, or when eval targets are missed. Include a summary of what happened and a link to the relevant planning artifact. Solves the async coordination problem on teams where the autonomous executor runs unattended.

items.title: Semantic Search Across Planning Artifacts
items.description: A /grd:search 'query' command that does semantic (not just keyword) search across all .planning/ markdown files. Returns ranked results with snippets. Solves the 'I know we decided something about this three phases ago but I can't find it' problem.

items.title: Phase Template Library
items.description: A library of reusable phase templates for common R&D patterns (benchmark evaluation, hyperparameter sweep, ablation study, literature review, dataset curation). /grd:add-phase --template ablation-study generates a correctly structured phase with pre-filled success criteria. Reduces blank-page friction.

items.title: Decision Audit Trail
items.description: Every design decision logged via `state add-decision` gets indexed with the phase it was made in, the alternatives considered, and the rationale. A /grd:decisions list command with filtering by topic or phase. Solves the 'why did we choose X over Y?' question that plagues long-running projects.

items.title: Metric Regression Detection
items.description: After each eval report, compare metrics against the BASELINE.md. If any tracked metric regresses beyond a configurable threshold, automatically create a blocker in STATE.md and optionally notify via webhook. Solves the silent regression problem where performance quietly degrades across phases.

items.title: Documentation Export (Confluence / Notion)
items.description: Export a milestone's planning artifacts as a structured document to Confluence or Notion: research findings, decisions, eval results, and final verification report. Solves the 'now write this up for stakeholders' step that researchers dread after completing R&D work.

items.title: Gradual Autonomy with Preference Learning
items.description: Track which confirmation prompts users routinely approve vs reject across sessions. Surface a 'you always approve X, want to automate it?' suggestion after 5+ approvals. Progressively expand YOLO scope based on demonstrated trust. Solves the tedium of re-approving the same low-risk actions repeatedly.

items.title: Auto-Generated PR Descriptions from Phase Plans
items.description: When a phase branch is ready to merge, automatically generate a rich PR description from the phase's PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, and eval results. Includes what was built, why, what was measured, and links to relevant planning artifacts. Eliminates the worst part of the merge process.

items.title: Compute Budget Planning
items.description: Let users annotate phases with estimated GPU hours / API calls. Track actuals from eval runs. Show a milestone-level compute budget view: estimated vs actual, remaining budget, and projected overage. Solves the 'we ran out of credits halfway through' problem on teams with fixed compute budgets.

items.title: AI Peer Review for Research Plans
items.description: A /grd:peer-review phase N command that spawns an adversarial agent to critique the phase's research methodology, flag confounders, question baselines, and suggest missing ablations — simulating a tough academic reviewer. Catches methodological flaws before expensive execution.

items.title: Changelog Auto-Writer
items.description: Generate a human-readable CHANGELOG entry for a completed milestone by synthesizing all phase SUMMARY.md files. Output user-facing language (not implementation jargon). Solves the 'writing changelogs is tedious and always delayed' problem that leads to sparse or missing release notes.

items.title: Phase Dependency Impact Analysis
items.description: When a phase is delayed or its scope changes, automatically compute which downstream phases are affected, by how many days, and what their revised start dates would be. Show a cascading impact view. Solves the 'one slipped phase ruins the whole milestone' planning problem.

items.title: Experiment Reproducibility Checker
items.description: Before marking a phase as verified, check that all experiments in EVAL.md have: a pinned random seed, pinned dependency versions, and a documented dataset split. Flag missing reproducibility artifacts as verification blockers. Solves the embarrassing 'I can't reproduce my own results' problem.

items.title: Phase-Level Team Assignment
items.description: Annotate phases and plans with assignees (GitHub usernames). When syncing to GitHub Issues or Jira, set issue assignees automatically. Show an assignment heatmap in the dashboard to surface overloaded team members. Solves the visibility gap on teams where multiple people contribute to a milestone.

items.title: R&D Knowledge Graph
items.description: Build a graph linking papers → decisions → phases → metrics → outcomes. Export as a Mermaid diagram or interactive HTML. Answers 'which paper led to which implementation decision that drove which metric improvement?' Solves the provenance problem that makes R&D hard to explain to others.

items.title: Phase Failure Autopsy
items.description: When a phase ends with missed eval targets or unresolved blockers, run an autopsy agent that reads all artifacts and produces a structured root cause analysis: what was expected, what happened, most likely cause, and what to try next. Removes the guesswork from 'why did this phase not work?'

items.title: Effort Estimation Calibration
items.description: Track estimated vs actual duration for every completed phase. Surface a calibration multiplier ('your estimates run 1.4x short on implementation phases, 0.9x on research phases'). Use this to auto-adjust future estimates. Solves the universal problem of software effort estimation being systematically wrong.

items.title: Backend Capability Parity Report
items.description: A /grd:backend-parity command that tests which GRD features are available under the current backend (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode) vs Claude Code, and which fall back gracefully. Renders a feature support matrix. Solves the 'does this work on my setup?' confusion when users switch backends.

items.title: Research Gap Detector
items.description: After a survey, compare what LANDSCAPE.md covers against the phase goals in ROADMAP.md. Highlight phases that reference techniques not covered in the survey, flagging them as 'research gaps' that need a deep-dive before planning. Prevents planning phases with insufficient research grounding.

items.title: VS Code Extension for GRD
items.description: A VS Code extension that renders the ROADMAP.md as a visual kanban board, highlights files touched by the active phase, and provides a sidebar for the dashboard. Solves the context-switching overhead between the terminal and the editor when tracking research progress.
